text.title: code-coverage Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for code-coverage as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Writing tests that execute code but contain no assertions (coverage gaming).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Lines are marked covered without any verification. Mutation testing (mutmut) reveals this: surviving mutants mean the code changed but tests still passed. The test calls mod.func() without asserting the return value or side effects, so the function's behavior is never verified.

failure-mode.description: Using # pragma: no cover on production business logic to hit a target.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: pragma: no cover should only exclude generated code, abstract stubs, and unreachable defensive guards. Reviewers must check every pragma in code review.

failure-mode.description: Measuring only repo total coverage instead of diff coverage on new code.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Legacy untested code drags total coverage down. Teams game the metric by writing trivial tests or marking exceptions. Diff coverage gates the thing the team controls: new code.

failure-mode.description: Running pytest --cov without omit = ["*/tests/*"] in coverage config.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: pytest-cov instruments test files themselves. Without omit, test lines inflate the total, hiding low coverage in production code.

failure-mode.description: Using --cov-append without awareness in parallel pytest runs (pytest-xdist).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each xdist worker writes a separate .coverage.N file. Without coverage combine, only the last worker's data is reported. Set parallel=True in .coveragerc and combine after the run.
